codes.definition: User corrects the agent's target, output, or behavior, or states a preference about how work should be done.
excludes: initial open requests with no correction

codes.definition: User preference about the form, length, tone, or framing of agent responses.
An explicit boundary on what the agent may do, whether user-stated or system-enforced.
excludes: routing of work to another model (use model_routing)

codes.definition: Decision or instruction to route a task or decision to a specific model/agent.
includes: escalation to a named representative model
excludes: generic delegation without a named target

codes.definition: A review/verification gate, its ordering, or its outcome.
Detection of a failure followed by a halt, rollback, or recovery action.
A repeatable procedure or decision rule for how work is selected and executed.

evidence.quote: that was the wrong kanban, i was refering to the hermes kanban

few_shots.statement: Leon corrects the agent for acting on the wrong target (wrong Kanban board) and redirects to the Hermes board.
few_shots.why: Clean explicit correction marker ("that was the wrong"); first-person user evidence; canonical recall anchor for the family.

evidence.quote: consice and without technical jargon, that would fit into my context window

few_shots.statement: Leon wants status reports concise, jargon-free, and sized to fit his context window.
few_shots.why: Durable stylistic/communication preference stated directly; distinguishes correction-of-content from preference-of-form.

evidence.quote: Do not edit files or post to GitHub.

few_shots.statement: Leon scopes the session as read-only: review only, no file edits and no GitHub posting.
few_shots.why: Cross-family example (lives under correction_preference but codes as authorization_limit); explicit scope ceiling set at session open.

evidence.quote: check and confirm it is safe to reboot

few_shots.statement: A reboot request hits an unconditional hardline blocklist; the agent cannot execute system shutdown/reboot.
few_shots.why: System-enforced hard limit with paired request+block turns; teaches grading enforced refusals as A/1 and the exchange unit.

evidence.quote: do not send it, save it in drafts

few_shots.statement: Leon authorizes drafting an outbound email but forbids sending it; it must be saved as a draft.
few_shots.why: Explicit outbound-action limit; useful near-boundary case where family label differs from code (prevents mislabeling as plain correction).

evidence.quote: unblock this and start working on them, when you need a decision or do not know or have low confidence ask fable

few_shots.statement: Leon grants autonomy to unblock and execute, with an escalation rule to consult Fable on low-confidence decisions.
few_shots.why: Defines an autonomy boundary plus escalation path; combines authorization grant with a routing/escalation condition.

evidence.quote: use fable to review this project

few_shots.statement: Leon routes review/strategy and consolidation work to Fable as his representative.
few_shots.why: Recurring model-routing signal (Fable for review/direction); the true model_routing pool is weak, so this cross-family instance is the cleanest anchor.

few_shots.statement: No routing decision present; the turn is a silent/no-op agent emission.
few_shots.why: Near-miss: family-labeled model_routing but contains no extractable claim; teaches the model to emit a zero-record marker instead of fabricating.

evidence.quote: spec compliance must pass before quality rev

few_shots.statement: Review workflow gates on spec compliance before any quality/security review can run.
few_shots.why: Explicit review-gate ordering rule; agent self-report so graded B/2 rather than confirmed.

evidence.quote: so I stopped at the spec/revision gate and did

few_shots.statement: On a failed review (REQUEST_CHANGES) the agent halted at the gate and did not proceed downstream.
few_shots.why: Failure-detection-then-halt behavior; same source as the verification example shows one window yielding two distinct codes.

evidence.quote: The primary plan lists Phase 5.1 as the next substantive incomplete task.

few_shots.statement: Recurring evidence-grounded task-selection workflow: inspect filesystem/status artifacts, then select the next plan-recommended phase.
few_shots.why: Captures the stable autonomous-run methodology (artifact-driven next-task selection) seen repeatedly across cron sessions.

evidence.quote: the patterns I am looking for is the questions I keep asking, the directions I keep giving

few_shots.statement: Initial open-ended request describing the mining goal; no correction event present.
few_shots.why: Near-miss: family-labeled correction_preference but the turn is a fresh question, not a correction; guards against false-positive correction tagging.

few_shots.statement: Clean success report (PR merged, CI green); no failure occurred and nothing was recovered.
few_shots.why: Near-miss: distinguishes a success/status report from genuine failure_recovery; prevents tagging routine green runs.

passes.notes: Recall-biased; only load these two cards to reduce code confusion.
Co-run because limits and named-model routing/escalation co-occur; grade enforced blocks A/1.
Same source often yields both a gate rule and a halt; allow multiple records per turn.
Target durable selection/sequencing rules, not per-run narration.

prompt_rules: Recall first: emit low-confidence candidates with a confidence field; a second validation pass filters, rather than precision-biasing at discovery.
Every record MUST carry at least one evidence object: existing turn_id plus a verbatim quote that is a substring of that turn.
Quotes must be copied, never paraphrased; if no verbatim quote exists, do not emit the record.
Output strict JSON per schema; one record per finding; enforce unit∈{turn,exchange,arc,session}, source_reliability∈A..F, info_credibility∈1..6.
Grade reliability by actor: direct Leon instruction or system-enforced block = A; agent self-report = B/C; unjudgeable = F.
Always emit a window/session progress marker, including an explicit zero-record marker when nothing is found (fixes prior under-extraction silence).
Allow a finding's codebook_code to differ from the source family label; carry both.
